author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Aries Native Description
text: Under Aries are born men Of thick hair, white or yellowish, curling; long visage, crooked nose, hazle eyes, little ears, short legs, thick feet: the first 15 degrees give a more gross body then the later.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Journey to King with Aquarius Moon
text: He that goes about any business to a King or Prince, when the Moon is in Aquarius, he may be received: but the King will not regard him, nor his Suit.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Gemini Native Description
text: Gemini gives a delicate, strait, well-composed, and well-made body, good colour, bright clear eyes, good sight, and piercing: long arms, long hands and feet, large breast, brown hair, good wit, fluent tongue, and apt discourse; yet a man of no great fidelity.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Journey to King with Pisces Moon
text: If the Moon be in Pisces, the King will not let him see his face.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Sagittarius Native Description
text: Under Sagittarius are born strait, well-proportioned bodies, strong and well-set; of a loving cheerful countenance, fair colour; good horse-men, and great shooters; stout, brown hair, and quickly bald.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Obtaining Desire
text: If any demand any thing of another (which may in Reason be granted) when Capricorn is in the Midheavens, and Jupiter in it, and the Moon apply to Jupiter, or if separating from him, she apply to the Lord of the Ascendant; or the Lord of the Ascendant apply to Jupiter, he will quickly obtain his desire.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Cancer Native Description
text: Cancer gives but a little short stature; yet the later fifteen degrees give a more full body then the former: you know one Sprat may be something bigger then another: the upper parts of the body are more thick then the lower; disorder in the teeth, little eyes, blackish hair, a low whining voice.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Leo Native Description
text: Leo arising, gives great head and eyes, quick sight, a large, lusty, strong body; full of metal, couragious, stout-hearted; thick, broad shoulders; yellowish or flaxen hair; a big great voice, resolute spirit, aspiring brain, of a generous, free-hearted, and courteous disposition.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Virgo Native Description
text: Virgo arising at a Nativity, gives a spare body, yet well composed; a mean stature, lovely brown complexion, great eyes, thick black hair, subtil wit, no great fidelity, aiming at self-ends.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Scorpio Native Description
text: Scorpio gives but short stature, yet a big-limbed, full, and well-set body, strong, and active; wilful, malicious, false, and deceitful; sad-brown hair, dark sallow complexion, short neck; quick in motion of body, but of reserved thoughts.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Libra Native Description
text: 'Twere pity to have missed Libra, the comeliest of person, and uprightest in condition of all the Zodiack. Libra gives a delicate, comely, strait body; a round, fair, and well-favoured face, hair for the most part flaxen or light-brown, but sometimes black: a cheerful, well-spoken creature; red lips, cherry-cheeks; a tall, slender body; courteous, impartial, and upright in all their dealings.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Moon's Light and Aspect for Journeys
text: The Moon increasing in light, in good Aspect to the Sun, helps forward Journeys.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Capricorn Native Description
text: Capricorn gives a very slender weakly man, dry, and lean; the face lean and thin; blackish hair, and it most commonly thick; a wan, obscure, or pale colour; a man as big about the middle as about the breasts; long neck, a disproportionate body; witty, cholerick; and sad.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Aquarius Native Description
text: Under Aquarius are born men of a middle stature, well shaped, clear skin, fair visage, yet more lovely than beautiful or curious, yet a handsome-composed body, a whitish-bright hair, and sometimes yellow.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Pisces Native Description
text: And lastly, Pisces gives a short, ill-composed body, yet a good face; of clear complexion and colour; thick shoulders, brown hair, fat and plump in all parts; an idle body, else no way hurtful.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Sun Native Description
text: In general descriptions of the Planets, consider, The Sun presents one of a brown or yellowish colour, round forehead, great hazle eyes, sharp and piercing, quick-sighted; of no great stature, yet fleshie, and full-boned: he hath a broad beard, and is quickly bald: a generous Creature, high-minded, aiming at no base or mean things.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Moon Native Description
text: The Moon declares one with a pale and whitely face, mixed with but little redness, if any: of mean stature, proud, of an auburn hair, slow of speech, fat, and phlegmatic; short arms, fleshy hands, of a mutable condition, often removing from place to place; a wearish, peevish creature, seldom contented; neither delighting much in action nor idleness.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Saturn Native Description
text: Saturn signifies a man with a black, swarthy colour, big and large eye-brows, black and little eyes, thin beard, and sometimes none at all; thick shoulders, and sometimes crooked; heavy, lumpish looks, tall stature: he goes with his head stooping forward: lean face, thick lips: he delights to be alone, and is always musing: he hits one leg against the other as he goes: he can hide his anger, and his tongue will tell no tales of his heart: wilful, envious, malicious, fearful, proud, and slovenly; covetous, aiming wholly at his own ends.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Jupiter Native Description
text: Jupiter represents a man tall of stature, of a brown ruddy complexion, an oval visage, high-forehead, fair gray eyes, light-brown hair, a handsome, well-set, strait body, large belly, great thighs, great well-proportioned legs, long feet, a sober, well-spoken, well-conditioned man, desiring to benefit all men; not covetous, nor caring for the goods of this world.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Mars Native Description
text: Mars describes one of an high colour, slender, yet strong, and well-set; a round sun-burnt face; his hair yellow or flaxen, and it curls; hazle eyes, quick, sharp, and piercing; furious looks; proud, presumptuous, valiant, full of words, boasting and lying.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Venus Native Description
text: Under Venus are born people of a whitely-brown colour and complexion; black and quick eyes, little eye-brows, red lips, cherry-cheeks with little dimples in them; the nether lip somewhat bigger than the upper; a neat, well-composed, and well-ordered body; strait, but of mean stature; affable, courteous, merry, wholly delighting in jollities.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Planet Description Modifiers
text: By comparing the Planet with the Sign he is in, the description of the body is found out. Also, by considering the strength of the Planet, his aspect to the Sun or Moon, his being with fixed Stars, either of, or opposing his own nature; with his being above or under the earth. For Planets, when they are above the earth, give far taller stature, than they do when they are under the earth.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Assailants' Provisions
text: The like you may judge of the Assailants, by the second House, which is the House of their substance.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Lord of 7th in 4th for Defendant
text: If the Lord of the seventh House be in the fourth, or the Lord of the fourth House in the seventh, it is good for the defendant, provided they are not aspected by Infortunes; for this one testimony is sufficient both in wars and law-suits.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Ascendant & 7th for Assailant
text: Judge the like by the Ascendant and seventh for the Assailant.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Lord of Ascendant in 8th
text: It is not good to fight when the Lord of the Ascendant is in the eighth House.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Sun in Ascendant or with Lord
text: The Sun doth no good in the Ascendant in the hour of battle, nor yet joined to the Lord of the Ascendant.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Assailant Loses Battle
text: The assailant always loseth the battle, if the Lord of the Ascendant be an Infortune; or retrograde, or combust.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Defendant Loses Battle
text: But if the Lord of the seventh be so, the defendant will be beaten, and he that begins the battle first, will have the victory.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: King Going to Battle
text: Let no King go to battle, when the Lord of the Ascendant applies to the seventh House.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Moon Application to Benefic
text: The application of the Moon shows the present state of the thing demanded: viz. To a good Planet, in a good house, by a good aspect, gives strong hopes of the thing desired.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Moon Application to Detriment
text: The application of the Moon to a Planet in his fall, or detriment, signifies trouble and delay in the business.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Retrograde Significator
text: If a Planet retrograde, or in his first station, be Significator, he denotes much discord and contradiction in the business.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Slow Significator
text: A Planet slow in motion, if he be Significator, prolongs mightily the thing sought after; especially, if he be in a sign contrary to his nature, or in the house of his enemy.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Infortunes' Effects
text: When the Infortunes signify evil, they bring it to pass with much violence; but if they signify good, they either bring it not to pass at all, or if they do, it comes with much delay, and vexation.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Infortunes Mitigated by Benefics
text: When the Infortunes signify any evil, see whether Jupiter or Venus cast friendly beams thither: if they do, they mitigate the evil; and so much the more, if it be with reception.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Fortunes Hindered by Malefics
text: When the Fortunes portend any good, if Saturn or Mars cast froward beams thither, without reception, it mightily hinders the good promised.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Fortune's Promise Reliability
text: Confide not too much upon what Fortune promiseth, unless he be either in essential dignities, or received by another that would fain do thee good, but want power.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Peregrine Malefics
text: Saturn and Mars peregrine, are malicious beyond measure: they are not so bad when they are in essential dignities; for then they are like noble enemies that have got their enemy in their hands, and scorn to hurt him.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Malefics in Dignity Promise Good
text: If Saturn or Mars be in their houses or exaltations, and in angles, and promise any good, they bring it to pass.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Defer Judgment
text: In a Question, when Fortunes and Infortunes are equally weak and ill-seated, defer the judgement till the heavens are better seated.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Planet Combust or Opposite Sun
text: When a Planet promising any good in a Question, is either in combustion with, or opposition to the Sun, he never bringeth the good to pass.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Saturn & Mars Conjunction
text: Saturn and Mars in conjunction; if they promise good, it never comes to pass: but if they promise evil, it comes with more malice than was expected.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Querent Hopeless
text: The Lord of the Ascendant, and the Moon peregrine, show the Querent is out of all hopes of his desire.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Significator in Sign Type
text: View whether the Significator of the thing desired be in a fixed sign: if he is, it will continue: if in a fickle sign, it will quickly end: if in a common sign, it will change out of one thing into another.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Lord of Ascendant/Moon with Nodes
text: If the Lord of the Ascendant, or the Moon, be with the head or tail of the Dragon, it brings damage. Look in what house they are, and from the signification of that house you may know whence the damage will come.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: First House Signification
text: The first house signifies the life, complexion, disposition, will, manners, and understanding of the Querent; the beginning of all enterprises.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Second House Signification
text: The second House signifies gain, traffique, gold, silver, and gain by traffique; all moveable goods without life.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Third House Signification
text: The third house signifies brethren, sisters, kindred, small voyages, short journeys, interpretation of dreams, etc.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Fourth House Signification
text: The fourth house signifies the father, houses, land, immoveable goods, orchards.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Judging Business by Separation Planet
text: You may know whether his business be good or bad; by the signification of the Planet he is separated from.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Business Effected by Significator Strength
text: And also, you may know whether his business be effected wholly, or in part; by the strength of the Significators.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Planet Impeding or Strengthening Significator
text: Also, you may note what Planet either impedites or strengthens the Significator; and so you may know the cause of it.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Lord of 10th for Honour Questions
text: If the Question be about Honour, take the Lord of the tenth house, and work accordingly.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Moon as Significatrix of Return
text: Another way to judge of returns, is this: See if the Moon be Lady of the hour of the Question: if so, and she be strong, she is Significatrix of the return: if she give beams to the Lord of the fifth house, he will return when she comes to his place, or perfect Aspect.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Moon Aspecting Ascendant for Return
text: But if she aspect the Ascendent, he will return when she comes to the degree ascending at the Question.

author: Nicholas Culpeper
title: Judgement Ruled by Reason
text: But let judgement herein be ruled by Reason; namely, if there be a possibility.
